title: Certified Phase Atlases for Biological ODE Models: Machine-Checkable Regime Classification via Validated Numerics, SMT Certificates, and Adaptive Parameter-Space Refinement

reviewers.name: Yuan Cheng
reviewers.key_strength: Clean separation between deterministic certified pipeline and optional GP exploration preserves soundness of all regime claims.
reviewers.key_weakness: No probabilistic framework for grading regime confidence near bifurcation boundaries, leading to binary certified/uncertified gaps that waste coverage.

reviewers.name: Aniruddha Sinha
reviewers.key_strength: Tiered verification architecture with graceful degradation (Tier 1 always, Tier 2/3 where feasible) is an exemplary design pattern for the formal-methods-for-science community.
reviewers.key_weakness: The scalability cliff from 2-state (100% coverage) to 3-state models (25% coverage on Repressilator) reveals exponential scaling that adaptive refinement does not adequately address.

reviewers.name: Lila Zhang
reviewers.key_strength: The delta-Soundness Bound provides a concrete, computable sufficient condition for when delta-complete verification implies exact regime correctness.
reviewers.key_weakness: Tier 2 (dReal) and Tier 3 (Z3) are not exercised in any benchmark, leaving the most distinctive SMT contributions entirely unvalidated.

reviewers.name: Sara Roy
reviewers.key_strength: GP-guided adaptive exploration correctly separates learning (where to look) from verification (what to certify) without compromising soundness.
reviewers.key_weakness: Minimal ML content—the optional GP layer is standard Bayesian optimization with no contribution to learning-augmented verification or the ML-verification intersection.

reviewers.name: Joseph S. Chang
reviewers.key_strength: MiniCheck (878 LoC) as an independent TCB verifier with 100% pass rate is the gold standard for building trust in certified computation results.
reviewers.key_weakness: The delta-Soundness Bound and Tier 3 exact verification are proven but not empirically exercised, leaving the strongest formal-guarantee claims unvalidated.

consensus_strengths: First system to produce machine-checkable certified phase diagrams for biological ODE models, establishing a new class of scientific artifact
Tiered verification architecture with graceful degradation guarantees every claim has at least one independent verification path
MiniCheck independent verifier (878 LoC) with 100% pass rate provides gold-standard TCB reduction
Hallucination audit in grounding.json demonstrates unusual intellectual honesty (48K to 10,760 LoC, 5 to 4 models, at least 95% to 84-100% coverage)
consensus_weaknesses: Tier 2 (dReal) and Tier 3 (Z3) SMT verification are not exercised in any benchmark, leaving the most distinctive contributions unvalidated
Coverage drops precipitously from 2-state models (84-100%) to 3-state models (25% on Repressilator), exposing exponential scaling in state dimension
No probabilistic framework for parameter uncertainty—deterministic set-membership approach wastes effort on low-probability parameter regions
Minimal ML content limits contribution to the learning-augmented verification community
Goodwin oscillator benchmark is missing from implementation, reducing the evaluation suite to 4 of 5 planned models
actionable_recommendations: Execute Milestone 0 dReal feasibility benchmarking immediately to validate or descope the SMT certificate generation pipeline
Implement anisotropic refinement guided by eigenvalue sensitivity to address the 25% coverage gap on the Repressilator and other 3-state models
Add a probabilistic qualification layer that assigns confidence levels to regime claims near bifurcation boundaries, converting binary gaps to graded assessments
Complete the Goodwin oscillator benchmark and add at least one DSGRN comparison to fulfill the evaluation plan commitments
Consider a learned refinement strategy (e.g., predicting promising cells from eigenvalue patterns across previous certifications) to improve scalability without compromising soundness
